name: Camero Viejo Loop from Ribafrecha
name_original: Camero Viejo – circular desde Ribafrecha – La Rioja Baja
description: Cycle 34.9 miles through Camero Viejo, tackling 3773 feet of elevation on this challenging touring route from Ribafrecha.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Ribafrecha traverse a diverse landscape in the autonomous region of La Rioja, Spain. The area is characterized by extensive vineyards and agricultural lands, particularly grain and olive fields, which offer scenic backdrops for cycling. Ribafrecha is situated at an elevation of approximately 498 to 501 meters, providing views of the surrounding countryside. The region also features mountains, forests, and river valleys, including the dramatic Leza River Canyon with vertical walls up to 200 meters high. Best touring cycling routes around Ribafrecha The most popular touring cycling route is The Vineyards of Rioja Media – circular from, a 14.0 miles (22.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through expansive vineyards and agricultural fields. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Camero Viejo Loop from Ribafrecha, a difficult 34.9 miles (56.2 km) path. This route showcases the rugged beauty of the Camero Viejo region, offering varied terrain and significant elevation changes. Local touring cyclists also love the Ruta de las Ermitas – circular from Lagunilla de Jubera by Leza de Río Leza, a 16.6 miles (26.8 km) trail leading through river valleys and agricultural areas, often completed in about 2 hours 11 minutes. Touring cycling around Ribafrecha is defined by rolling vineyards, river valleys, and the dramatic Leza River Canyon. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides through agricultural lands to more challenging routes in the mountainous terrain. The routes in Ribafrecha are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 3,900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ribafrecha's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Ribafrecha?
A: The touring cycling routes around Ribafrecha offer a diverse range of terrain. You'll find picturesque rides through extensive vineyards and agricultural lands, particularly grain and olive fields, which are characteristic of the Mid-Rioja region. There are also routes that venture into mountainous areas, forests, and river valleys, including the dramatic Leza River Canyon with its vertical walls. This mix provides options from gentle, rolling paths to more challenging ascents and descents.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Ribafrecha?
A: Yes, Ribafrecha offers touring cycling routes suitable for various skill levels. While there are 20 difficult routes, there are also 7 easy and 18 moderate routes. These easier options often wind through the agricultural lands and vineyards, providing a more relaxed experience for beginners or families looking for a scenic ride without significant elevation challenges. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995647343/the-vineyards-of-rioja-media-circular-from" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Vineyards of Rioja Media – circular from&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that takes you through expansive vineyards.

Q: What are some of the natural highlights or landmarks I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Cycling around Ribafrecha allows you to experience several natural and historical highlights. A prominent natural feature is the Leza River Canyon, part of a Biosphere Reserve, known for its impressive vertical walls and diverse wildlife, including griffon vultures. You might also encounter historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2706737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clavijo Castle&lt;/a&gt; or explore areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iregua Park&lt;/a&gt;. The routes often pass through authentic villages and offer views of the surrounding mountains, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4054053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte La Pila&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5136513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laturce&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Ribafrecha and its surroundings offer several difficult routes. These routes often feature significant elevation changes and varied terrain. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994478593/camero-viejo-loop-from-ribafrecha" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camero Viejo Loop from Ribafrecha&lt;/a&gt;, a 34.9-mile path with substantial elevation gain that showcases the rugged beauty of the Camero Viejo region. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994449613/ruta-de-las-ermitas-circular-from-lagunilla-de-jubera-by-leza-de-rio-leza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de las Ermitas – circular from Lagunilla de Jubera by Leza de Río Leza&lt;/a&gt;, which also includes considerable climbing.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Ribafrecha?
A: The best time to go touring cycling in Ribafrecha is generally during the spring and autumn months. During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, ideal for longer rides. The vineyards, a prominent feature of the landscape, are particularly beautiful as they change color in the autumn or burst with new growth in the spring. Summer can also be suitable, but be prepared for warmer temperatures, especially in the midday sun.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in the Ribafrecha area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Ribafrecha are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995647343/the-vineyards-of-rioja-media-circular-from" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Vineyards of Rioja Media – circular from&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994449613/ruta-de-las-ermitas-circular-from-lagunilla-de-jubera by Leza de Río Leza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de las Ermitas – circular from Lagunilla de Jubera by Leza de Río Leza&lt;/a&gt;. These loops are convenient for exploration, bringing you back to your starting point.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Ribafrecha?
A: The touring cycling routes in Ribafrecha are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 170 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from the rolling vineyards to the rugged beauty of the Leza River Canyon and the Sierra de Cameros. The region's quiet country roads and well-maintained trails, offering a mix of paved and gravel surfaces, are also frequently highlighted for providing tranquil and scenic rides.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the cycling routes in Ribafrecha?
A: Yes, the La Rioja region, including areas around Ribafrecha, is known for integrating cultural and gastronomic experiences with outdoor activities. You'll find opportunities to visit authentic villages, enjoy renowned Riojan cuisine, and taste local wines. While specific cafes or accommodations directly on every route aren't listed, the proximity to villages and towns means you can easily find places to refuel and rest during or after your ride.

Q: Is it possible to reach Ribafrecha by public transport for cycling trips?
A: Ribafrecha is located relatively close to Logroño, the capital of La Rioja, which serves as a key transport hub. While direct public transport options to Ribafrecha specifically for cyclists with bikes might be limited, Logroño offers connections that could facilitate reaching the region. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or regional train services from Logroño for the most up-to-date information on transport options that accommodate bicycles.

Q: Are the touring cycling routes in Ribafrecha suitable for gravel bikes or are they mostly paved?
A: The region around Ribafrecha offers a mix of surfaces suitable for various bike types. While many routes utilize quiet secondary roads that are paved, the area also features peaceful trails and agricultural paths that are well-suited for gravel bikes. This diversity allows cyclists to choose routes that match their preferred bike type and riding experience, from smooth tarmac to more rugged, unpaved sections through vineyards and natural areas.

Q: What is the total number of touring cycling routes available around Ribafrecha?
A: There are over 45 touring cycling routes available around Ribafrecha, offering a wide variety of distances, difficulties, and scenic experiences. This extensive network ensures that cyclists of all levels can find a route that suits their preferences, whether they are looking for an easy ride through vineyards or a challenging adventure through mountainous terrain.
tips:
Small town located at an altitude of 719 meters in the Leza river basin, in the Camero Viejo region.
Formerly known for its textile industry, currently the small population that still lives in the municipality is mainly dedicated to livestock. It stands out for its renowned marzipans made in the town.
Spectacular views of the Leza Canyon. The space is conditioned with a bench so that visitors can observe both the mountainous relief and the flora and fauna of the place from the viewpoint.

The Vulture viewpoint is located in an old quarry, on the Vía Verde del Cidacos, less than 1km from Arnedillo. From this point there are beautiful views of the enormous limestone rocks, as well as being able to enjoy the colony of griffon vultures that inhabit the area, where they take advantage of the thermal currents to move through the air.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult and demands a very good fitness level. It involves significant elevation gain and some sections may be unpaved, potentially requiring you to push your bike. It's best suited for experienced touring cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied terrain, including mountainous relief with significant unevenness. While some parts of La Rioja offer easier, paved stages, this challenging route in the Camero Viejo region may feature unpaved segments. Be prepared for both paved and potentially rougher surfaces.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular views and passes by several notable points. You'll experience the rugged beauty of the Camero Viejo region, with highlights such as the town of Soto en Cameros, the stunning Leza Canyon Viewpoint, and the Mirador del Buitre. You'll also see panoramic views of towns and the Sierra de Cameros.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: La Rioja generally offers a pleasant climate for cycling. Considering the mountainous terrain and potential for unpaved sections, spring and autumn are often ideal, providing comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery without the extreme heat of summer or the chill of winter. Always check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Camero Viejo touring bicycle route from Ribafrecha?
faqs.answer: As the route starts from Ribafrecha, it's advisable to look for parking within or near the town center. Specific designated parking for cyclists might not be explicitly marked, so plan to use public parking areas available in Ribafrecha.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is not suitable for beginners. It is classified as 'difficult' and requires a very good fitness level due to its significant elevation gain and challenging terrain. Beginners should look for easier routes in La Rioja, perhaps along the Ebro river.

faqs.question: Is this a loop route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as indicated by its name 'circular from Ribafrecha', this is a loop route. You will start and finish in Ribafrecha.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You will encounter sections of official trails such as CIMA LR05 Santa Marina, Camino de Soto a Santa Marina, Camino a Santa Marina, Pista a Cerro la Muela, Senda del Piojo, Cno de Santa Cecilia, and Camino a Treguajantes.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this particular touring bicycle route are not detailed, it's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash in natural areas, especially given the presence of wildlife and potential for livestock. Always ensure your dog can handle the demanding terrain and duration.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route through the Camero Viejo region. It's an open touring bicycle route accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or water sources along the way?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through towns like Soto en Cameros, which may offer amenities, the mountainous areas of Camero Viejo can be isolated. It's highly recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire duration of the ride, as facilities might be sparse between settlements.
